results.title: Girls in schools
results.creator_credit: Luigi Acquisto and Australia. Dept. of Employment, Education and Training
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Australian mosaic”.
Made by SBS for the Department of Employment, Education and Training in Canberra, as part of its Gender Equity Network initiative in support of the National Policy for the Education of Girls in Australian schools.

results.title: Clive eats alligators
results.creator_credit: Jeff Spencer, David Ferris and Victoria. Schools Division. Curriculum Branch
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Cover to cover”.
Trevor Lunn interviews children’s writer Alison Lester about her children’s books particularly “Clive eats alligators”. She dicusses with children from South Melbourne Primary School her reasons and inspirations for writing and the choice of illustrations in her childrens’ books.

results.title: Jane Tanner
results.creator_credit: Jeff Spencer, Lily Steiner, TV Ed Productions and Victoria. Ministry of Education
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Cover to cover”.
Jane Tanner is a Melbourne-based illustrator of children’s books, best known for her illustrations in “Time for a Rhyme” and “There’s a Sea in my Bedroom”. She and Allan Baillie, who writes the text for children’s books, are interviewed by Trevor Lunn. Jane discusses her career, what she thinks makes a good picture book and displays examples of her work as she explains her working methods. She and Allan then discuss collaborating on books.

results.title: Blood: what is it?
results.creator_credit: Jeff Spencer, Jim Mann and TV Ed Productions
results.brief_description: Margaret Brumby from the Monash University and Peter Carolan from the Blood Bank explains the composition of blood, tests and procedures of the Blood Bank and discuss related social issues. The program also shows how a blood smear is made as well as a blood group testing.

results.title: Mr Wasinski's Song
results.brief_description: While visiting an ear doctor Mr Wasinski discovers that his deafness is linked to a frightening childhood experience. During his consultation the doctor discovers gauze buried deep within his ear canal and upon its removal Mr. Wasinski becomes vulnerable to a barrage of repressed childhood memories. Having lived as a child in Poland during Nazi occupation Mr. Wasinski’s deafness marks the loss of his innocence as a child when separated from his parents during a desperate attempt to escape. The vivid memories suppressed with the aid of his deafness prove a challenge to an aging man who seeks distance from his childhood experiences. Stars Bruce Kerr as Mr Wasinski.

results.title: Confessions of a Simple Surgeon
results.creator_credit: Paul Harmon and Educational Division of N S W Dept of TAFE
results.brief_description: This humorous presents the associated health risks of smoking and passive smoking in an often graphic but factual manner. “Confessions” calls for a total ban on tobacco promotion and its message to viewers is clear. If you smoke, quit. If you don’t , don’t be conned into smoking and avoid being a victim of passive smoking. It is presented by Dr Arthur Chesterfield-Evans, whose experience in surgery on cancer victims led him to become an activist in BUGAUP (Billboard Utilising Graffitists Against Unhealthy Promotions) and in the legal activities of the Non-Smokers Movement of Australia.
